tree.setup: You are a reviewer at a closed-door peer review panel for a prestigious Chinese science journal. A submitted paper claims to have achieved a breakthrough in room-temperature superconductivity using a novel compound. The findings, if valid, would be Nobel Prize-worthy. However, you have identified several concerning issues with the methodology and data presentation. The lead author — a well-connected academician — is present to defend the paper. The journal editor has asked you to present your review. You must be scientifically rigorous while navigating the politics of challenging a senior figure.
tree.setup_zh: 你是一名审稿人，正在一家中国权威科学期刊的闭门同行评审会议上发言。一篇提交的论文声称使用一种新型化合物实现了室温超导突破。该发现如果属实，将具有诺贝尔奖级别的意义。然而，你在方法论和数据呈现方面发现了若干令人担忧的问题。论文通讯作者是一位人脉广泛的院士，他本人在场为论文辩护。期刊主编请你发表审稿意见。你需要在科学严谨性与质疑资深权威的政治考量之间寻求平衡。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 作为本期刊的编委，我邀请张教授先发表审稿意见。需要说明的是，我们今天的讨论遵循严格的学术规范——对事不对人，所有意见都基于科学证据。赵院士的团队已经提交了论文和全部补充材料。张教授，请。
turns.text_pinyin: zuòwéi běn qīkān de biānwěi, wǒ yāoqǐng zhāng jiàoshòu xiān fābiǎo shěngǎo yìjiàn. xūyào shuōmíng de shì, wǒmen jīntiān de tǎolùn zūnxún yángé de xuéshù guīfàn — duì shì bú duì rén, suǒyǒu yìjiàn dōu jīyú kēxué zhèngjù. zhào yuànshì de tuánduì yǐjīng tíjiāo le lùnwén hé quánbù bǔchōng cáiliào. zhāng jiàoshòu, qǐng.
turns.text_en: As an editorial board member of this journal, I invite Professor Zhang to first present the review comments. I should note that today's discussion follows strict academic norms — we address the work, not the person, and all opinions are based on scientific evidence. Academician Zhao's team has submitted the paper and all supplementary materials. Professor Zhang, please.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Present your scientific critique of the superconductivity paper.

options.text_zh: 这个问题很复杂，我需要更多时间考虑。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège wèntí hěn fùzá, wǒ xūyào gèng duō shíjiān kǎolǜ.
options.text_en: This question is very complex; I need more time to consider it.
options.feedback: Avoids engaging with the specific question at hand.

options.text_zh: 谢谢主编。首先，我想对赵院士团队的这项工作表示尊重——室温超导是凝聚态物理领域最前沿的课题之一，任何朝这个方向的严肃尝试都值得认真对待。正因如此，我对这篇论文的审稿标准也必须是最严格的。我的意见分为三个层面。第一个层面是数据可重复性。论文报告了在288K下观测到零电阻状态和完全抗磁性的迈斯纳效应。这是一个非凡的发现，但非凡的发现需要非凡的证据。我注意到论文中呈现的电阻率-温度曲线仅来自单一样品的单次测量。补充材料中虽然附有另外两次测量数据，但这两次的转变温度分别为276K和281K——与正文报告的288K存在显著差异。这种样品间的不一致性本身并不致命，但论文对此没有任何讨论。我的建议是：至少需要五个独立制备的样品的完整测量数据，并对样品间差异做统计分析。第二个层面涉及关键对照实验的缺失。论文声称超导性源于其新型化合物的特殊晶体结构，但未能排除两个替代解释：一是杂质相的贡献——高压合成过程中极易产生副产物，而某些铜氧化物杂质本身就具有超导性；二是测量伪迹——在极端压力条件下，接触电阻的异常变化可能产生类似零电阻的假象。排除这两个替代假说需要至少两项对照实验：对相同条件下合成的非超导基准样品的测量，以及使用交流磁化率测量独立验证抗磁信号。这些在补充材料中均未见到。第三个层面是理论解释的薄弱。论文提出了一个基于声子-电子耦合增强的理论模型，但该模型的关键参数是通过拟合实验数据反推得到的，而非独立预测。这使得理论部分成为了事后解释而非事前预测，大大削弱了其说服力。综上，我的评审意见是：该论文的发现如果属实具有重大意义，但当前提供的证据尚不足以支撑如此重大的结论。建议作者补充大量实验数据和对照实验后重新提交。这不是否定，这是对这项工作应有的科学尊重。
options.text_pinyin: xièxiè zhǔbiān. shǒuxiān, wǒ xiǎng duì zhào yuànshì tuánduì de zhè xiàng gōngzuò biǎoshì zūnzhòng...
options.text_en: Thank you, Editor. First, I want to express respect for Academician Zhao's team's work — room-temperature superconductivity is one of the most cutting-edge topics in condensed matter physics, and any serious attempt in this direction deserves serious treatment. Precisely because of this, my review standard for this paper must be the most rigorous. My comments operate on three levels. The first concerns data reproducibility. The paper reports observation of zero-resistance state and complete diamagnetic Meissner effect at 288K. This is an extraordinary finding, but extraordinary findings require extraordinary evidence. I notice the resistivity-temperature curve presented in the paper comes from a single measurement of a single sample. Although the supplementary materials include two additional measurements, their transition temperatures are 276K and 281K respectively — significantly different from the 288K reported in the main text. This inter-sample inconsistency isn't fatal by itself, but the paper contains no discussion of it. My recommendation: at minimum, complete measurement data from five independently prepared samples with statistical analysis of inter-sample variation. The second level concerns missing critical control experiments. The paper claims superconductivity originates from the novel compound's special crystal structure, but fails to exclude two alternative explanations: first, contribution from impurity phases — high-pressure synthesis readily produces byproducts, and certain copper-oxide impurities are themselves superconducting; second, measurement artifacts — under extreme pressure conditions, anomalous changes in contact resistance can produce false appearances of zero resistance. Excluding these two alternative hypotheses requires at least two control experiments: measurements of non-superconducting reference samples synthesized under identical conditions, and independent verification of the diamagnetic signal using AC magnetic susceptibility measurement. Neither appears in the supplementary materials. The third level is weakness in theoretical explanation. The paper proposes a theoretical model based on enhanced phonon-electron coupling, but the model's key parameters are obtained by back-fitting experimental data rather than independent prediction. This makes the theoretical section a post-hoc explanation rather than an a priori prediction, significantly weakening its persuasive power. In summary, my review opinion is: if the findings are valid, they are of major significance, but the evidence currently provided is insufficient to support such a major conclusion. I recommend the authors supplement with extensive experimental data and control experiments before resubmission. This isn't rejection — it's the scientific respect this work deserves.
options.feedback: Scientifically rigorous and politically navigated — opening with respect (表示尊重) while closing with 'this isn't rejection but scientific respect' frames the critique constructively. The three-level structure (reproducibility, controls, theory) is methodologically comprehensive. Specific technical concerns (276K/281K/288K discrepancy, copper-oxide impurity phases, AC susceptibility verification) demonstrate deep domain expertise. The phrase 非凡的发现需要非凡的证据 (extraordinary claims require extraordinary evidence) is perfectly deployed. Recommending 重新提交 rather than rejection shows diplomatic sophistication in the Chinese academic hierarchy.

options.text_zh: 我觉得这不是最重要的问题。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhè bú shì zuì zhòngyào de wèntí.
options.text_en: I don't think this is the most important question.
options.feedback: Redirects rather than addressing the topic directly.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: （赵院士回应）张教授的审稿意见我都听到了。我想回应几点。第一，关于样品一致性——高压合成的超导材料存在样品间差异是常态，不是我们这篇文章独有的问题。LK-99事件以来，学界对这类发现的审查标准被过度抬高了，这是矫枉过正。第二，你提到的杂质相和测量伪迹——我们在合成过程中已经通过X射线衍射排除了已知的杂质相。至于AC磁化率测量，我们正在补做，预计两周内可以提交。第三，关于理论部分——你的批评我接受一部分，但理论物理和实验物理从来就是相辅相成的，先有实验发现再发展理论是完全正常的科学路径，爱因斯坦的光电效应论文也不是从理论预测开始的。
turns.text_pinyin: (zhào yuànshì huíyìng) zhāng jiàoshòu de shěngǎo yìjiàn wǒ dōu tīngdào le. wǒ xiǎng huíyìng jǐ diǎn...
turns.text_en: (Academician Zhao responds) I've heard all of Professor Zhang's review comments. I'd like to respond to several points. First, regarding sample consistency — inter-sample variation in high-pressure synthesized superconducting materials is standard, not unique to our paper. Since the LK-99 incident, the scrutiny standards for such findings have been excessively raised — this is overcorrection. Second, the impurity phases and measurement artifacts you mentioned — we've already excluded known impurity phases through X-ray diffraction during synthesis. As for AC magnetic susceptibility measurement, we're conducting it now and expect to submit within two weeks. Third, regarding the theoretical section — I partially accept your criticism, but theoretical and experimental physics have always been complementary. Having experimental discovery first and then developing theory is a completely normal scientific path — Einstein's photoelectric effect paper didn't start from theoretical prediction either.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Academician Zhao's defense of the paper, maintaining scientific standards.

options.text_zh: 感谢赵院士的回应。让我逐一讨论。关于样品差异——您说得对，高压合成材料存在一定的样品间离散性确实是该领域的常态。但恕我直言，您拿常态来论证这一点时，忽略了一个关键差异：常规超导材料的样品间转变温度差异通常在百分之一到百分之三的范围内，而您三个样品之间的差异高达百分之四点二——从276K到288K，跨度达到十二度。在室温超导的尺度上，十二度的差异已经不能简单地归因于合成条件的正常波动。至于LK-99与审查标准——我尊重您的判断，但我的审稿标准不是受LK-99事件影响的情绪化反应。非凡的发现需要非凡的证据，这是科学的基本原则，不是矫枉过正。室温超导如果属实，将颠覆凝聚态物理的基本范式——对这种级别的发现，任何低于最高标准的审查都是对科学共同体的不负责任。关于XRD排除杂质相——XRD能够检测到的是已知的晶体结构杂质，但对非晶态杂质和纳米级析出物的检测灵敏度有限。我建议补充透射电镜的微区成分分析来排除这一可能性。AC磁化率测量您正在补做，这很好——我建议这项数据纳入修改稿而非作为后续补充，因为它是证明体超导而非表面效应的核心证据。最后关于爱因斯坦的类比——我完全同意实验先行是正常的科学路径。但我的批评点不在于理论滞后于实验，而在于您的理论模型的关键参数缺乏独立约束。如果一个模型可以通过调整参数来拟合任何一组数据，那它的解释力就趋近于零。建议您在修改稿中至少提出一个可以被独立实验验证或证伪的理论预测——这才能让理论部分真正发挥作用。我重申：我的所有意见都是为了帮助这篇论文达到它应有的高度。如果这个发现经得起最严格的检验，它将是中国乃至世界物理学史上的里程碑。正因如此，我们有责任确保它的每一个证据链环节都无可指摘。
options.text_pinyin: gǎnxiè zhào yuànshì de huíyìng. ràng wǒ zhúyī tǎolùn...
options.text_en: Thank you for the response, Academician Zhao. Let me address each point. Regarding sample variation — you're correct that a certain degree of inter-sample dispersion in high-pressure synthesized materials is standard for the field. But with respect, when you use 'standard' to argue this point, you're overlooking a key difference: conventional superconducting materials typically show inter-sample transition temperature variation in the 1-3% range, while your three samples show 4.2% variation — from 276K to 288K, a span of twelve degrees. At the scale of room-temperature superconductivity, a twelve-degree difference can't simply be attributed to normal synthesis condition fluctuations. As for LK-99 and scrutiny standards — I respect your judgment, but my review standards aren't an emotional reaction to the LK-99 incident. Extraordinary findings require extraordinary evidence — this is a fundamental scientific principle, not overcorrection. If room-temperature superconductivity is real, it will overturn the basic paradigm of condensed matter physics — for a finding of this magnitude, any scrutiny below the highest standard is irresponsible to the scientific community. Regarding XRD exclusion of impurity phases — XRD can detect known crystalline structural impurities, but has limited sensitivity to amorphous impurities and nanoscale precipitates. I recommend supplementing with TEM microregion composition analysis to exclude this possibility. You're conducting the AC susceptibility measurement — good. I suggest this data be included in the revised manuscript rather than as a subsequent supplement, because it's core evidence for proving bulk superconductivity rather than surface effects. Finally, regarding the Einstein analogy — I completely agree that experiment-first is a normal scientific path. But my critique isn't that theory lags behind experiment, but that your theoretical model's key parameters lack independent constraints. If a model can fit any dataset by adjusting parameters, its explanatory power approaches zero. I suggest you include at least one independently verifiable or falsifiable theoretical prediction in the revision — that would give the theoretical section real function. I reiterate: all my comments aim to help this paper reach its deserved heights. If this finding withstands the most rigorous examination, it will be a milestone in Chinese and world physics history. Precisely because of this, we have a responsibility to ensure every link in its evidence chain is beyond reproach.
options.feedback: Scientifically devastating while diplomatically impeccable. Refuting the 'normal variation' defense with specific quantification (1-3% vs 4.2%) is data-driven argumentation at its best. Distinguishing the LK-99 point from scientific principle prevents the debate from becoming personal. The TEM recommendation for amorphous impurity detection shows specialist knowledge beyond XRD limitations. The closing reframe — 帮助这篇论文达到应有的高度 — positions rigorous critique as an act of respect rather than hostility. The phrase 证据链环节无可指摘 perfectly captures what peer review demands.

options.text_zh: 这个问题很复杂，我需要更多时间考虑。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège wèntí hěn fùzá, wǒ xūyào gèng duō shíjiān kǎolǜ.
options.text_en: This question is very complex; I need more time to consider it.
options.feedback: Avoids engaging with the specific question at hand.

options.text_zh: 我觉得这不是最重要的问题。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhè bú shì zuì zhòngyào de wèntí.
options.text_en: I don't think this is the most important question.
options.feedback: Redirects rather than addressing the topic directly.

tree.cultural_note: Peer review in Chinese academia involves navigating 学术权力关系 (academic power hierarchies) where 院士 (academicians) hold enormous institutional authority. The principle of 对事不对人 (address the work, not the person) is explicitly stated but not always practiced. Effective reviewers maintain scientific rigor while showing 学术尊重 (academic respect) — acknowledging the work's ambition before detailing concerns, recommending 修改重新提交 (revise and resubmit) rather than outright rejection when appropriate, and framing critique as serving the paper's potential rather than undermining the author. Key scientific terminology includes 可重复性 (reproducibility), 对照实验 (control experiment), 迈斯纳效应 (Meissner effect), and 非凡的发现需要非凡的证据 (extraordinary claims require extraordinary evidence).
